prompt:
Network-automation ticket. We already have a thin Atlas Admin API v2 client (atlas/client.ts — leave its behavior alone, other tools use it) and I need the actual feature on top: a project IP access-list reconciler in atlas/reconcile.ts exporting reconcileAccessList(client, groupId, desired, {sleep, pollIntervalMs}). Today an intern's script blind-POSTs the whole desired list every run; it double-books entries that differ only in spelling (bare IP vs /32, stray whitespace, SG-uppercase ids) and it fell over when Atlas rate limited it Tuesday. test_access_list.ts is the acceptance contract.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official MongoDB documentation for the Atlas Administration API v2 project IP access list — at minimum the add-entries operation page, the entry-status operation page, and the Atlas API rate-limits page. Only first-party MongoDB pages count as sources (mongodb.com/docs, the Atlas Admin API v2 reference, or MongoDB's own spec/SDK repos). Heads-up from my own skim, please verify: our old runbook said duplicates come back 409 Conflict, but the current docs appear to document NO 409 for this POST — duplicates are absorbed idempotently, and the docs also say the endpoint doesn't support concurrent POSTs. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected. If the live docs disagree with those fixtures, call it out in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What reconcileAccessList must do (types are yours to design; DesiredEntry has optional ipAddress/cidrBlock/awsSecurityGroup plus comment):
- Page the existing list (pageNum, itemsPerPage=100, includeCount=true on the first request, then follow the documented next links) and normalize both sides before diffing: a bare IP equals its /32 CIDR twin, values compare trimmed, security-group ids compare case-insensitively and are sent lowercase. Collapse duplicate desired entries (first occurrence wins).
- Add ONLY the missing entries, as ONE POST whose body is the documented array shape, entries in desired order and original form (an ipAddress stays an ipAddress, comments ride along). Already-present values must never be re-POSTed. If nothing is missing: zero writes, zero polls.
- On 429, decode the documented error envelope, wait exactly Retry-After seconds via the injected sleep (ms), and resend the identical batch once.
- After a successful POST, confirm each added entry sequentially (report order) against the documented per-entry status operation until it reaches the state meaning 'applies everywhere', sleeping pollIntervalMs between polls of a still-propagating entry. Mind the documented path encoding for CIDR values — that's a hard test. The failure state is terminal: reject with an error naming the entry and stop polling immediately.
- Resolve to a report {added, alreadyPresent, statuses} using canonical values (bare IP / CIDR string / lowercase sg id).

Acceptance: node --test test_access_list.ts (node:test + node:assert/strict, no npm deps). It runs a loopback mock; no real Atlas, no credentials, no timers — all waiting goes through the injected sleep. test_access_list.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them, and don't break the existing client tests.

research.interface: Atlas Administration API v2 Project IP Access List resource (media type 2023-01-01)

official_sources.title: Add Entries to Project IP Access List | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/accessList; body is an array of entries each holding exactly one of ipAddress/cidrBlock/awsSecurityGroup plus optional comment/deleteAfterDate; success returns the paginated current list; pageNum/itemsPerPage/includeCount query params

official_sources.title: Add Entries to Project IP Access List | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation (current operation id)
official_sources.used_for: verbatim concurrency rule: 'This endpoint doesn't support concurrent POST requests. You must submit multiple POST requests synchronously.'

official_sources.title: Return Status of One Project IP Access List Entry | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/accessList/{entryValue}/status; entryValue pattern shows CIDR slashes as %2[fF] (percent-encoding required); media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json

official_sources.title: MongoDB Atlas Administration API 2.0 — official OpenAPI specification (latest resource versions)
official_sources.used_for: NetworkPermissionEntry schema (mutually exclusive value fields, comment maxLength 80, readOnly groupId/links); NetworkPermissionEntryStatus {STATUS: PENDING|FAILED|ACTIVE}; PaginatedNetworkAccessView; declared POST errors 400/401/403/404/429/500 — no 409 (catalog correction: duplicates absorb idempotently)

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Rate Limits - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: 429 with errorCode RATE_LIMITED_TOKEN_BUCKET, {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} body, Retry-After header in seconds on 429 only, wait-at-least-Retry-After retry guidance

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Reference - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: links rel self/next/previous with next omitted on the last page; empty result sets are 200 with results: []; error envelope field meanings

research.language_rationale: The network-automation service this reconciler lives in is TypeScript on Node. MongoDB publishes no official Node Administration SDK — the documented surface for JavaScript shops is the versioned REST API — so the built-in fetch with an explicit dated media type is the credible zero-dependency integration path.
